title: Deparia subsimilis
taxonomy: Deparia subsimilis is a species of fern in the family Athyriaceae. It is a type of evergreen fern native to East Asia. The genus Deparia includes several species of ferns, which are commonly found in forests and woodlands. Deparia subsimilis is closely related to other species in the genus, such as Deparia acrostichoides and Deparia japonica. The classification of Deparia subsimilis is still being studied by botanists and taxonomists, who continue to refine our understanding of this and other fern species.

intro: Deparia subsimilis, also known as the slender lady fern, is a species of fern belonging to the genus Deparia. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, and is traditionally used to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Deparia subsimilis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
